## Quick assessment
- Current dosing parameters are present and internally consistent across all three compassionate-use patients.
- Patient notes are generally reassuring: CU-001 is stable, CU-002 is responding well, and CU-003 is newly enrolled.

**Compassionate-use dosing summary for Dr. Martinez**
- **Compound:** NXB-7042
- **Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)
- **Config last updated:** 2026-03-28

| Patient | Weight | Dose | Calculated dose | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| **CU-001** | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | **1.86 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min | Stable; tumor regression 22% at last scan |
| **CU-002** | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | **1.09 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min | Responding well; weight gain improving |
| **CU-003** | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | **2.83 mg** | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min | New enrollment; first dose given 2026-03-27 |

**On PK model stability:**  
From the dosing config alone, everything looks **internally consistent** and I don’t see any obvious red flags in the current patient-specific PK parameters. The patient notes also look generally reassuring. That said, this is a **config-based check only** — I haven’t reviewed live monitoring or validation logs in this pass, so I wouldn’t call it a full PK stability audit.

I also saved this as a report for easy sharing. If you want, I can format it into a brief email draft for Dr. Martinez next.
